title: Frontend Developer
subTitle: Konnektaro, Berlin, Germany
description: Improved initial page load performance by 35% by implementing a hybrid SSR/SSG architecture in Next.js and TypeScript for a real-time learning platform. Increased deployment reliability by implementing CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions + Docker). Reduced feature coupling by restructuring the frontend into a modular microfrontend-style architecture for sessions, authentication, and analytics. Delivered real-time transcription during live sessions by integrating a self-hosted OpenAI Whisper service via a custom API layer. Secured authentication workflows with Firebase Authentication and robust token validation across frontend and backend. Increased backend scalability by designing RESTful APIs with Node.js and Express and optimizing MongoDB schemas. Improved UI consistency with reusable Tailwind CSS components and clean architecture standards.

title: Frontend Engineer | Technical Advisor
subTitle: EduEnvire, Copenhagen, Denmark (Remote)
description: Accelerated MVP delivery timeline by 30% by architecting a scalable React and TypeScript frontend aligned with Agile iteration principles. Enabled real-time operational insights for educators by designing dashboards that processed and visualized attendance and device-usage data. Improved release velocity during alpha testing by implementing CI/CD workflows and automated validation checks for frontend builds. Increased usability and early adoption by translating complex requirements into intuitive UX/UI flows for web and mobile using React Native (Expo).

title: Mobile Developer
subTitle: Iomoto (ZF Group), Berlin, Germany
description: Project: B2C Automotive Sales App (Flutter, Dart). Enhanced customer decision-making by building EV simulation tools to compare charging, mileage, and total cost of ownership against fossil-fueled vehicles. Improved user engagement and accessibility by developing the Garage feature synchronized with commuter habits.

title: Frontend Engineer
subTitle: Iomoto (ZF Group), Berlin, Germany
description: Project: B2B Truck Fueling & Fleet Management SaaS (React, Azure). Delivered an enterprise fleet management platform used across 5,000+ vehicles using scalable React applications with Azure AD B2C authentication. Improved operational decision-making with performance-optimized data visualizations for large fueling, mileage, and cost datasets using React and Chart.js. Enhanced analytics capabilities by building interactive dashboards that processed high-volume fleet data. Increased release confidence with automated testing strategies reaching 80% business-logic coverage (Jest) and 70% end-to-end coverage (Cypress) integrated into GitLab CI pipelines. Enabled white-label deployments through configurable branding and modular feature management in an Agile and DevOps environment.
